tool_input.summary: Reconnection in Saturn’s magnetotail is most often identified (from Cassini in situ signatures) in the central plasma sheet/current sheet of the nightside tail, near the midnight sector, typically in the near-to-mid tail at roughly ~20–40 RS (Saturn radii) downtail. The datasets below (MAG + plasma/particles + SPICE geometry) are the core PDS holdings used to locate and quantify those reconnection signatures.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated Cassini MAG vectors in Saturn-tail coordinate systems (e.g., KRTP/KSM) used to detect reconnection signatures (plasmoid flux ropes, dipolarizations) and to map event locations in the tail.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw/high-time-resolution MAG measurements to examine detailed reconnection-associated field rotations and current sheet crossings.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Ion/electron plasma distributions to identify plasma sheet intervals, flows, and heating associated with reconnection in the magnetotail.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived plasma moments/spacecraft potential products useful for automated surveys of tail reconnection-related flows and density changes.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic ion/electron observations (CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS) used to detect particle injections and energetic signatures associated with tail reconnection.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Electron density time series helps distinguish lobe vs plasma sheet and contextualize reconnection intervals in the tail.

candidates.title: CASSINI SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels for spacecraft ephemeris/attitude needed to compute Cassini’s position (X,Y,Z in KSM/KSO etc.) and bin reconnection occurrence versus downtail distance/local time.

candidates.title: CO S MIMI 4 CHEMS CALIB V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3-era calibrated MIMI/CHEMS energetic particle measurements—useful if reproducing older reconnection/event studies based on PDS3 volumes.

candidates.title: CO S MIMI 4 INCA CALIB V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3-era calibrated MIMI/INCA energetic neutral atom / energetic ion context for magnetotail dynamics tied to reconnection.

candidates.title: CO S MIMI 4 LEMMS CALIB V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3-era calibrated MIMI/LEMMS energetic particle measurements that can show reconnection-associated injections.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.

dataset.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes 1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute averaged data is provided as well.

dataset.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle
dataset.description: The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime mission. The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47 UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers data received during extended missions. Sampling rates were variable and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities on-board.

The datasets below (MAG + plasma/particles + SPICE geometry) are the core PDS holdings used to locate and quantify those reconnection
